text: إِنَّ عَلِيّاً ( عليه السلام ) بَابٌ مِنْ أَبْوَابِ الْجَنَّةِ فَمَنْ دَخَلَ بَابَهُ كَانَ مُؤْمِناً وَ مَنْ خَرَجَ مِنْ بَابِهِ كَانَ كَافِراً وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَدْخُلْ فِيهِ وَ لَمْ يَخْرُجْ مِنْهُ كَانَ فِي الطَّبَقَةِ الَّتِي لِلَّهِ فِيهِمُ الْمَشِيئَةُ .

en.hubeali: (It has been narrated) from Abu Ibrahimasws (7th Imamasws) having said that Aliasws is a door from the doors of the Paradise. So the one who enters hisasws door would be a Momin, but the one who exits from hisasws door would be a Unbeliever (Kafir), and the one who does not enter into it and does not exit from it would be in the category whose (fate would depend on) Allahazwj Desire (Decision) regarding them’.
en.sarwar: 21. Yunus has narrated from Musa ibn Bukayr from abu Ibrahim (a.s.) who has said the following: “Ali (a.s.) is a door of the doors of paradise. Whoever approaches through him is a believer, those who depart him are unbelievers, and those who do not approach or leave are among the group about whom Allah has the will to decide.”
